planning_review.notes: The remaining queue had the live-route probe and frontend acceptance root test. Codex selected the live-route probe because its URL/auth and HTTP/HTML helper boundaries were explicit, while the frontend acceptance file is a test aggregate with higher coverage-regression risk.

extracted_modules.domain: header registration, shared edge auth token resolution, route classification, project mount URL construction, and mounted-route checks
HTTP/HTTPS requests, bounded redirect following, auth redirect detection, Workbench HTML marker detection, JSON detection, and mounted asset extraction

post_extraction_review.notes: Read-only bounded review found no auth, redirect, route-validation, import-direction, or test-weakening regression in the extraction.

verification_notes: The first full npm test run failed one workbench-server-shard-06 scheduler-resume assertion, while large-file, diff, and closeout gates passed.
The failed shard was rerun directly and passed.
A second full npm test run passed 1013/1013, so the initial failure was treated as transient and not as a P35 extraction regression.
